title: BREACH LAB Base Filesystem
description: Shared Linux training environment used by all BREACH LAB scenarios.

README.txt.content:
BREACH LAB — Cybersecurity Training Terminal

  challenges/    CTF missions (6 levels)
  tools/         Security tooling scripts
  notes/         Reference notes
  .bash_history  Command history

Help: help
Missions: cd challenges && cat README.txt

CTF MISSIONS — Beginner to Advanced

  01-recon/    System reconnaissance     [EASY]
  02-perms/    File permissions          [EASY]
  03-hidden/   Hidden files              [MEDIUM]
  04-grep/     Text analysis             [MEDIUM]
  05-privesc/  Privilege escalation      [HARD]
  06-network/  Network analysis          [HARD]

Each mission folder contains a mission.txt brief.
When you complete the required steps, the flag is revealed automatically in the terminal — no manual file read required.

TOOLS:
  nmap.sh       → Port scanner simulator
  linpeas.sh    → Privesc check
  hashcrack.py  → MD5 cracker (demo)

Usage: bash <tool.sh>

nmap.sh.content:
#!/bin/bash
echo "[*] Nmap Simulated Scan"
echo "PORT     STATE  SERVICE  VERSION"
echo "22/tcp   open   ssh      OpenSSH 8.2p1"
echo "80/tcp   open   http     nginx 1.18.0"
echo "443/tcp  open   https    nginx 1.18.0"
echo "3306/tcp open   mysql    MySQL 8.0.26"
echo "8080/tcp open   http-alt Node.js"
echo "[+] Scan complete — 5 open ports"

linpeas.sh.content:
#!/bin/bash
echo "╔══════════ LinPEAS v4.5.2 ══════════╗"
echo "[+] SUID/SGID files:"
echo "  /usr/bin/sudo  /usr/bin/passwd  /usr/bin/newgrp"
echo "[+] Writable directories:"
echo "  /tmp  /var/tmp  /dev/shm"
echo "[+] Sudo configuration:"
echo "  operator ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/vim"
echo "[!] Warning: vim can spawn a root shell."
echo "  sudo vim -c '!bash'"

linux-commands.txt.content:
BASIC LINUX COMMANDS

FILES:   ls -la | cat | grep | find | chmod | chown
TEXT:    grep | awk | sed | wc | head | tail | sort
SYSTEM:  ps aux | top | uname -a | id | env | which
NET:     ifconfig | netstat | ss | ping | curl | wget
CTF:     strings | xxd | base64 | file | binwalk

ctf-checklist.txt.content:
CTF STARTER CHECKLIST

[ ] file <target>            → File type
[ ] strings <target>         → Readable strings
[ ] xxd <target> | head      → Hex dump
[ ] find / -name "*.txt"     → Search by extension
[ ] env | grep -i token      → Environment variables
[ ] cat ~/.bash_history      → Command history
[ ] sudo -l                  → Sudo privileges
[ ] find / -perm -4000       → SUID files
[ ] crontab -l               → Scheduled tasks

.bash_history.content:
ls -la
cd challenges
cat mission.txt
grep -r FLAG .
sudo -l
find / -perm -4000 2>/dev/null
cat /etc/passwd
netstat -tulpn
